texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Introduction
texts.text: 1. Introduction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Different membranes have been prepared and studied for applications in separating gas components, such as CO2 from CH4, O2 from N2, natural gas and gasoline from mixture gas stream, for both valuable chemical recovery/separation [1-3] and emission control [4-11], such as in synthetic fiber or automobile industry [12-14], at lab-scale or pilot-scale and industrial scale [4,15]. Volatile organic compounds (VOC) separation efficiency is mainly affected by solubility and diffusion rate, the polymer property and penetrates property and Flory-Huggins parameters that symbolize their interaction [16-18]. Recently, thin layer polyether block amide (PEBA) on polymer of vinylidene fluoride (PVDF) hollow fiber [12], capable of achieving 95% gasoline vapor recovery was reported. Rubbery membranes, especially polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) membranes, have high separation factor for VOC components than permanent gases like O2 and N2 , had been studied extensively [4-9]. For reducingmembraneresistanceandincreasingselectivity,thisPDMSlayer was difficult to be made very thin and uniform, such as in alumina PDMS hollow fiber membrane [11]. Besides this dense layer, a recent study indicates that the porous support layer resistance
texts.text: Different membranes have been prepared and studied for applications in separating gas components, such as CO2 from CH4, O2 from N2, natural gas and gasoline from mixture gas stream, for both valuable chemical recovery/separation [1-3] and emission control [4-11], such as in synthetic fiber or automobile industry [12-14], at lab-scale or pilot-scale and industrial scale [4,15]. Volatile organic compounds (VOC) separation efficiency is mainly affected by solubility and diffusion rate, the polymer property and penetrates property and Flory-Huggins parameters that symbolize their interaction [16-18]. Recently, thin layer polyether block amide (PEBA) on polymer of vinylidene fluoride (PVDF) hollow fiber [12], capable of achieving 95% gasoline vapor recovery was reported. Rubbery membranes, especially polydimethylsiloxane (PDMS) membranes, have high separation factor for VOC components than permanent gases like O2 and N2 , had been studied extensively [4-9]. For reducingmembraneresistanceandincreasingselectivity,thisPDMSlayer was difficult to be made very thin and uniform, such as in alumina PDMS hollow fiber membrane [11]. Besides this dense layer, a recent study indicates that the porous support layer resistance

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: a b s t r a c t
texts.text: a b s t r a c t

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Membrane with polyoctylmethyl siloxane (POMS) separation layer, in flat and spiral wound module, was tested to remove toluene from air by applying vacuum pressure at the permeate side of the membrane. Effect of vacuum pressure (0.02-0.1 MPa), feed flow rate (0.6-3.0 × 10 -2 m 3 /h, linear velocity across the membrane at 4.3-21.5m 3 /(m 2 h)) and toluene concentration (1500-4000mg/m 3 , common for industrial effluents) on removal efficiency of the flat membrane module was determined. Under similar feed concentration and vacuum pressure, a higher toluene removal efficiency was obtained using the spiral wound membrane module than using the flat membrane, when flow rates in spiral wound unit ranged 0.15-0.27 m 3 /h (linear velocities across the membrane at 2.0-3.15 m 3 /(m 2 h), much lower than in flat membrane). Under 0.09MPa vacuum, toluene removal in spiral wound unit ranged between 70 and 84%. The removal percentage of toluene did not change much when its concentration increased in feed. This result shows that the membrane used to recover toluene in effluent stream can achieve high removal efficiency.
texts.text: Membrane with polyoctylmethyl siloxane (POMS) separation layer, in flat and spiral wound module, was tested to remove toluene from air by applying vacuum pressure at the permeate side of the membrane. Effect of vacuum pressure (0.02-0.1 MPa), feed flow rate (0.6-3.0 × 10 -2 m 3 /h, linear velocity across the membrane at 4.3-21.5m 3 /(m 2 h)) and toluene concentration (1500-4000mg/m 3 , common for industrial effluents) on removal efficiency of the flat membrane module was determined. Under similar feed concentration and vacuum pressure, a higher toluene removal efficiency was obtained using the spiral wound membrane module than using the flat membrane, when flow rates in spiral wound unit ranged 0.15-0.27 m 3 /h (linear velocities across the membrane at 2.0-3.15 m 3 /(m 2 h), much lower than in flat membrane). Under 0.09MPa vacuum, toluene removal in spiral wound unit ranged between 70 and 84%. The removal percentage of toluene did not change much when its concentration increased in feed. This result shows that the membrane used to recover toluene in effluent stream can achieve high removal efficiency.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: may present a significant resistance to diffusion [16]. Even so, a new kind of POMS membrane based on OMS monomer rather than DMS had been synthesized. Tested in extracting hydrocarbon from aqueous solutions, better result was achieved using POMS than using PDMS membrane [1], but it has not been reported for VOC gas emission control yet. So, it was tested in this work for gas phase VOC recovery and emission control using toluene as a model pollutant.
texts.text: may present a significant resistance to diffusion [16]. Even so, a new kind of POMS membrane based on OMS monomer rather than DMS had been synthesized. Tested in extracting hydrocarbon from aqueous solutions, better result was achieved using POMS than using PDMS membrane [1], but it has not been reported for VOC gas emission control yet. So, it was tested in this work for gas phase VOC recovery and emission control using toluene as a model pollutant.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In most industrial operations, to force gas component permeate through the membrane, compressing at the feed side and vacuuming at the permeate side is often adopted [15]. But in environmental protection and emission control, some gas streams are usually under atmospheric pressure, of low pollutant concentration and possibly with explosive components, it prohibits gas compression, thus only vacuuming at the permeate side is possible for creating the required trans-membrane pressure. In this case, with a reduction in energy consumption, the driving force is lowered comparing with using both compression and vacuum. Study carried out this way is seldom reported. In this study, only vacuum was used to create a driving force for testing POMS composite membranes, both in a permeation cell for flat membrane, and in a tubular module for the spiral wound membrane, to separate and recover toluene, a common atmospheric pollutant from air. The aim is to evaluate the separation performance of the POMS membrane, and supply optimal operation parameters for applications.
texts.text: In most industrial operations, to force gas component permeate through the membrane, compressing at the feed side and vacuuming at the permeate side is often adopted [15]. But in environmental protection and emission control, some gas streams are usually under atmospheric pressure, of low pollutant concentration and possibly with explosive components, it prohibits gas compression, thus only vacuuming at the permeate side is possible for creating the required trans-membrane pressure. In this case, with a reduction in energy consumption, the driving force is lowered comparing with using both compression and vacuum. Study carried out this way is seldom reported. In this study, only vacuum was used to create a driving force for testing POMS composite membranes, both in a permeation cell for flat membrane, and in a tubular module for the spiral wound membrane, to separate and recover toluene, a common atmospheric pollutant from air. The aim is to evaluate the separation performance of the POMS membrane, and supply optimal operation parameters for applications.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Experimental
texts.text: 2. Experimental

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.1. Membrane and membrane modules
texts.text: 2.1. Membrane and membrane modules

texts.label: text
texts.orig: POMS composite membrane is provided by Euro-film (Dalian, China). It consists of three layers: a polyester (PET) support layer, a polyacrylonitrile (PAN) microporous layer (less than 50 𝛍 m), and the top active separation layer polyoctylmethyl siloxane (POMS, less than 1 𝛍 m). In principle, its preparation is similar to the polyester/PAN/PDMS membrane described in Ref. [16].
texts.text: POMS composite membrane is provided by Euro-film (Dalian, China). It consists of three layers: a polyester (PET) support layer, a polyacrylonitrile (PAN) microporous layer (less than 50 𝛍 m), and the top active separation layer polyoctylmethyl siloxane (POMS, less than 1 𝛍 m). In principle, its preparation is similar to the polyester/PAN/PDMS membrane described in Ref. [16].

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.1.1. Preparation of base membrane
texts.text: 2.1.1. Preparation of base membrane

texts.label: text
texts.orig: PolymerofPANwasdissolvedinDMFsolvent,formingasolution to be used for casting using a flat membrane casting machine. After being cast on polyester nonwoven, the membrane was immersed in gelation water basin, inside, there was a fast exchange of solvent with water, the liquid polymer in cast layer turned into a solid gel, and finally solidified on the nonwoven with certain strength.
texts.text: PolymerofPANwasdissolvedinDMFsolvent,formingasolution to be used for casting using a flat membrane casting machine. After being cast on polyester nonwoven, the membrane was immersed in gelation water basin, inside, there was a fast exchange of solvent with water, the liquid polymer in cast layer turned into a solid gel, and finally solidified on the nonwoven with certain strength.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.1.2. Surface dense layer preparation
texts.text: 2.1.2. Surface dense layer preparation

texts.label: text
texts.orig: CoatingsolutioncontainingPOMSprecursorwasputinastorage tank, then only the side of the base membrane with PAN layer was immersedinthetank,thusathinlayerwascoated.Later, the coated base membrane was heated in a drying oven. A heat curing process was used to polymerize and solidify the POMS precursors. A transparent rubbery dense layer on the base membrane was formed. Then the composite membrane for VOC recovery was ready.
texts.text: CoatingsolutioncontainingPOMSprecursorwasputinastorage tank, then only the side of the base membrane with PAN layer was immersedinthetank,thusathinlayerwascoated.Later, the coated base membrane was heated in a drying oven. A heat curing process was used to polymerize and solidify the POMS precursors. A transparent rubbery dense layer on the base membrane was formed. Then the composite membrane for VOC recovery was ready.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Twoflatmembranesandtwospiralwoundmodulesweretested, where the active separation layer thickness was different, M1 thinner than M2, both less than 1 𝛍 m. Flat membranes were tested in a permeate cell made of stainless steel, with an effective surface area of 14.1 cm 2 . Installed spiral wound membrane surface area in a stainless steel casing is 0.08 m 2 for M1 and 0.096m 2 for M2.
texts.text: Twoflatmembranesandtwospiralwoundmodulesweretested, where the active separation layer thickness was different, M1 thinner than M2, both less than 1 𝛍 m. Flat membranes were tested in a permeate cell made of stainless steel, with an effective surface area of 14.1 cm 2 . Installed spiral wound membrane surface area in a stainless steel casing is 0.08 m 2 for M1 and 0.096m 2 for M2.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.2. Simulate waste gas streams and experimental setup
texts.text: 2.2. Simulate waste gas streams and experimental setup

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The schematic setup of the membrane extractor is shown in Fig. 1(a). One portion of air entered the vaporizing bottle filled with toluene and became saturated with toluene vapor; it was then mixed with the other section of air forming feed gas mixture, toluene concentration was controlled by changing the mixing ratios using rotameter. The mixed gas stream was introduced into the membrane unit. The toluene concentration in feed stream was 1500mg/m 3 ( ± 7%) or 3000mg/m 3 ( ± 5%), and 4000mg/m 3 ( ± 5%), simulating solvent/VOC emission levels in operations in auto industry and synthetic fiber industry [13,14]. The feed gas flow rates into permeate cell varied between 0.6 and 1.8 × 10 -2 m 3 /h, and the feed gas flow rates for spiral wound module were 0.15m 3 /h and 0.27 m 3 /h. The flow rates of retentate gas were measured. Feed stream was at atmospheric pressure and the vacuum applied at the permeate side was 0.02-0.1MPa for flat membrane 0.02-0.09MPa for spiral wound modules. The concentration of toluene in feed stream and retentate gas was monitored and analyzed with Gas Chromatography (Shimadzu GC-14C, FID detector). The tests were carried out at 17 ± 2 ◦ C. The effluent toluene concentration was used to determine the driving force, the difference between the partial pressure in feed gas and permeate gas. It is used for calculating J using the equation J i = q/A/SOHp i .
texts.text: The schematic setup of the membrane extractor is shown in Fig. 1(a). One portion of air entered the vaporizing bottle filled with toluene and became saturated with toluene vapor; it was then mixed with the other section of air forming feed gas mixture, toluene concentration was controlled by changing the mixing ratios using rotameter. The mixed gas stream was introduced into the membrane unit. The toluene concentration in feed stream was 1500mg/m 3 ( ± 7%) or 3000mg/m 3 ( ± 5%), and 4000mg/m 3 ( ± 5%), simulating solvent/VOC emission levels in operations in auto industry and synthetic fiber industry [13,14]. The feed gas flow rates into permeate cell varied between 0.6 and 1.8 × 10 -2 m 3 /h, and the feed gas flow rates for spiral wound module were 0.15m 3 /h and 0.27 m 3 /h. The flow rates of retentate gas were measured. Feed stream was at atmospheric pressure and the vacuum applied at the permeate side was 0.02-0.1MPa for flat membrane 0.02-0.09MPa for spiral wound modules. The concentration of toluene in feed stream and retentate gas was monitored and analyzed with Gas Chromatography (Shimadzu GC-14C, FID detector). The tests were carried out at 17 ± 2 ◦ C. The effluent toluene concentration was used to determine the driving force, the difference between the partial pressure in feed gas and permeate gas. It is used for calculating J using the equation J i = q/A/SOHp i .

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Result and discussions
texts.text: 3. Result and discussions

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.1. Toluene separation by flat membranes
texts.text: 3.1. Toluene separation by flat membranes

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.1.1. Effect of feed gas flow rate on toluene recovery
texts.text: 3.1.1. Effect of feed gas flow rate on toluene recovery

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Test results proved that increasing feed gas flow rate, led to a decrease in toluene removal from simulate streams, such as from 0.6 × 10 -2 m 3 /h to 3 × 10 -2 m 3 /h. At a flow rate of 0.6 × 10 -2 m 3 /h (linear velocity at 4.3 m 3 /(m 2 h) across the membrane), the removal rate of toluene from gas stream was 38-79%, but when flow rate increased, the removal rates decreased (maximal 36-42%), when the operating pressure difference was fixed in the range of 0.02-0.09 MPa (Fig. 1(b)). While increasing feed gas flow further to (1.8-3.0) × 10 -2 m 3 /h, linear velocity increased from 12.9 to 21.5 m 3 /(m 2 h), a bit too higher than normally used (around 4-7m 3 /(m 2 h)) [15], so the removal rate became even lower (10-40%) and the difference between toluene removal at different flow rates became narrower, as permeance was becoming steady. The permeated toluene was only a portion of toluene in the feed gas, this portion depended on operating pressure and flow rate, as high flow rate reduced resident/contact time of gas stream with the membrane in separation unit, it led to a reduction in toluene removal. At low flow rate, a relatively higher ratio of toluene gets dissolved in the membrane, and permeates through the membrane, so the removal of toluene is higher than at high flow rate.
texts.text: Test results proved that increasing feed gas flow rate, led to a decrease in toluene removal from simulate streams, such as from 0.6 × 10 -2 m 3 /h to 3 × 10 -2 m 3 /h. At a flow rate of 0.6 × 10 -2 m 3 /h (linear velocity at 4.3 m 3 /(m 2 h) across the membrane), the removal rate of toluene from gas stream was 38-79%, but when flow rate increased, the removal rates decreased (maximal 36-42%), when the operating pressure difference was fixed in the range of 0.02-0.09 MPa (Fig. 1(b)). While increasing feed gas flow further to (1.8-3.0) × 10 -2 m 3 /h, linear velocity increased from 12.9 to 21.5 m 3 /(m 2 h), a bit too higher than normally used (around 4-7m 3 /(m 2 h)) [15], so the removal rate became even lower (10-40%) and the difference between toluene removal at different flow rates became narrower, as permeance was becoming steady. The permeated toluene was only a portion of toluene in the feed gas, this portion depended on operating pressure and flow rate, as high flow rate reduced resident/contact time of gas stream with the membrane in separation unit, it led to a reduction in toluene removal. At low flow rate, a relatively higher ratio of toluene gets dissolved in the membrane, and permeates through the membrane, so the removal of toluene is higher than at high flow rate.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.1.2. Effect of trans-membrane pressure (TMP) on toluene removal
texts.text: 3.1.2. Effect of trans-membrane pressure (TMP) on toluene removal

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The effect of TMP on toluene removal is shown in Figs. 1(b) and 2(a). In Fig. 1(b) when feed flow rate was 1.8-3.1 × 10 -2 m 3 /h, the removal rates increased with the increase of TMP, especially when TMP was 0.08-0.1 MPa. This is also shown in Fig. 2(a), as expected from the permeation theory. As TMP rise means a bigger driving force/higher gradient for toluene diffusion, a higher removal rate may be obtained.
texts.text: The effect of TMP on toluene removal is shown in Figs. 1(b) and 2(a). In Fig. 1(b) when feed flow rate was 1.8-3.1 × 10 -2 m 3 /h, the removal rates increased with the increase of TMP, especially when TMP was 0.08-0.1 MPa. This is also shown in Fig. 2(a), as expected from the permeation theory. As TMP rise means a bigger driving force/higher gradient for toluene diffusion, a higher removal rate may be obtained.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: But when flow rate was 0.6 × 10 -2 m 3 /h, as shown in Fig. 1(b), the increase of removal Y was not obvious, (even slight decrease, considering the error ranges 2-12.5%)when operating pressure /Delta1 P increased from 0.02 to 0.06MPa. Y increased quite significantly when /Delta1 P increased from 0.06 to 0.09 MPa.
texts.text: But when flow rate was 0.6 × 10 -2 m 3 /h, as shown in Fig. 1(b), the increase of removal Y was not obvious, (even slight decrease, considering the error ranges 2-12.5%)when operating pressure /Delta1 P increased from 0.02 to 0.06MPa. Y increased quite significantly when /Delta1 P increased from 0.06 to 0.09 MPa.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This phenomenon could be due to the extremely short and direct connection to the vacuum pressure gauge and the cooler to condense toluene. Increasing vacuum pressure might lead to the reduction of efficiency of toluene condensation in the cooler, thus toluene partial pressure might have increased. Immediate vacuuming in the flat membrane module configuration and change of condensing efficiency adversely affected toluene diffusion as TMP increased from 0.02 to 0.06 MPa.
texts.text: This phenomenon could be due to the extremely short and direct connection to the vacuum pressure gauge and the cooler to condense toluene. Increasing vacuum pressure might lead to the reduction of efficiency of toluene condensation in the cooler, thus toluene partial pressure might have increased. Immediate vacuuming in the flat membrane module configuration and change of condensing efficiency adversely affected toluene diffusion as TMP increased from 0.02 to 0.06 MPa.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Once concentration gradient in POMS and PET re-established and became stable, toluene diffusion rate increased positively with operating pressure, an increased stable transport of toluene across the membrane took place with increase of TMP.
texts.text: Once concentration gradient in POMS and PET re-established and became stable, toluene diffusion rate increased positively with operating pressure, an increased stable transport of toluene across the membrane took place with increase of TMP.

texts.text: Increasing flow rate further to 1.2 × 10 -2 m 3 /h (8.6 m 3 /(m 2 h)) or above, this phenomena of decrease of removal rate with the increase of TMP became less obvious, even negligible, within range of statistical error.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In Fig. 2(a), the toluene removal efficiency for both M1 and M2 increased slowly when /Delta1 P increased from 0.02 to 0.06 MPa, and toluene removal was higher when thinner membrane M1 was used. A greater increase was seen when /Delta1 P increased from 0.06 to 0.09MPa.
texts.text: In Fig. 2(a), the toluene removal efficiency for both M1 and M2 increased slowly when /Delta1 P increased from 0.02 to 0.06 MPa, and toluene removal was higher when thinner membrane M1 was used. A greater increase was seen when /Delta1 P increased from 0.06 to 0.09MPa.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The toluene permeance J across membrane is also bigger for thinner membrane M1 than for M2, maximal difference is about 0.8-1.6 × 10 -7 mols -1 m -2 Pa -1 , significantly higher than the error range. This means thinner membrane is better than thicker one for gas permeation. As shown in Fig. 2(b), the permeance J ranged between 1.4 × 10 -7 and 6 × 10 -7 mols -1 m -2 Pa -1 (feed toluene at 1500 mg/m 3 ). This value was lower than J 2.08-7.10 × 10 -7 mols -1 m -2 Pa -1 obtained at higher flow rate (1.8 × 10 -2 m 3 /h) when toluene in the feed was also 1500 mg/m 3 . The statistical error range/standard relative deviation was 1.7-12.5% for toluene permeance J (in most cases error range less than 7%).
texts.text: The toluene permeance J across membrane is also bigger for thinner membrane M1 than for M2, maximal difference is about 0.8-1.6 × 10 -7 mols -1 m -2 Pa -1 , significantly higher than the error range. This means thinner membrane is better than thicker one for gas permeation. As shown in Fig. 2(b), the permeance J ranged between 1.4 × 10 -7 and 6 × 10 -7 mols -1 m -2 Pa -1 (feed toluene at 1500 mg/m 3 ). This value was lower than J 2.08-7.10 × 10 -7 mols -1 m -2 Pa -1 obtained at higher flow rate (1.8 × 10 -2 m 3 /h) when toluene in the feed was also 1500 mg/m 3 . The statistical error range/standard relative deviation was 1.7-12.5% for toluene permeance J (in most cases error range less than 7%).

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Studies in the past showed that the VOC concentration in feed will influence the separation results, e.g. the recovery of toluene via membrane separation by Schultz and Peinemann [1]. In this study, for toluene at 1500-4000mg/m 3 , the removal rates in flat membrane increased slightly as the toluene concentration increased in the feed. Also an increase was seen for toluene permeance. Increasing toluene concentration from 1500 to 3000mg/m 3 , an increase of J from 2.62 to 2.80 × 10 -7 mols -1 m -2 Pa -1 when TMP was 0.08MPa and an increase from 7.10 to 7.35 × 10 -7 mols -1 m -2 Pa -1 when TMP was 0.1MPa were determined (feed flow rate was 1.8 × 10 -2 m 3 /h).Peinemann
texts.text: Studies in the past showed that the VOC concentration in feed will influence the separation results, e.g. the recovery of toluene via membrane separation by Schultz and Peinemann [1]. In this study, for toluene at 1500-4000mg/m 3 , the removal rates in flat membrane increased slightly as the toluene concentration increased in the feed. Also an increase was seen for toluene permeance. Increasing toluene concentration from 1500 to 3000mg/m 3 , an increase of J from 2.62 to 2.80 × 10 -7 mols -1 m -2 Pa -1 when TMP was 0.08MPa and an increase from 7.10 to 7.35 × 10 -7 mols -1 m -2 Pa -1 when TMP was 0.1MPa were determined (feed flow rate was 1.8 × 10 -2 m 3 /h).Peinemann

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.2. Separation of toluene from air using spiral wound modules
texts.text: 3.2. Separation of toluene from air using spiral wound modules

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In testing with spiral wound module, the linear velocity of gas stream at 2.0-2.5m 3 /(m 2 h) was much slower than that in flat membrane. Removal of toluene from simulate waste stream under different conditions (pressure, flow rate and concentration) was studied. Shown in Fig. 3(a) and (b), were toluene removal rates and permeance J , respectively. Increase of the operating pressure had a positive effect on removal rate and permeance J . The increase of permeance J was linear to the increase of TMP.
texts.text: In testing with spiral wound module, the linear velocity of gas stream at 2.0-2.5m 3 /(m 2 h) was much slower than that in flat membrane. Removal of toluene from simulate waste stream under different conditions (pressure, flow rate and concentration) was studied. Shown in Fig. 3(a) and (b), were toluene removal rates and permeance J , respectively. Increase of the operating pressure had a positive effect on removal rate and permeance J . The increase of permeance J was linear to the increase of TMP.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In spiral wound membrane, highest toluene removal reached 84%. Permeance J reached 8.01 × 10 -7 mols -1 m -2 Pa -1 . These values were higher than those in flat membrane. The calculated gas linear velocity is 2.0-2.8 mh -1 in spiral membrane, lower than 4.3-13 or 13-21mh -1 in flat membrane. So spiral wound membrane had higher removal efficiency in recovering VOC from waste streams. This comparison also showed the potential of POMS spiral wound membrane for toluene recovery and treatment at higher flow rates.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3.2.1. Comparison of permeance J in flat membrane and spiral membrane
texts.text: 3.2.1. Comparison of permeance J in flat membrane and spiral membrane

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In spiral wound and/or flat membrane module, the permeance of toluene is in the range of 1.30 × 10 -7 to 8.01 × 10 -7 mols -1 m -2 Pa -1 (Table 1). The calculated corresponding linear velocity to the membrane area for flat membrane was 12.7 mh -1 , much higher than that (2.87 m h -1 ) in spiral membrane, when operating pressure difference was low (0.02-0.04MPa), the values of J for flat and spiral membrane were similar, but when the operating pressure difference increased to 0.08 MPa, the value of J was much higher for spiral membrane than for flat membrane.
texts.text: In spiral wound and/or flat membrane module, the permeance of toluene is in the range of 1.30 × 10 -7 to 8.01 × 10 -7 mols -1 m -2 Pa -1 (Table 1). The calculated corresponding linear velocity to the membrane area for flat membrane was 12.7 mh -1 , much higher than that (2.87 m h -1 ) in spiral membrane, when operating pressure difference was low (0.02-0.04MPa), the values of J for flat and spiral membrane were similar, but when the operating pressure difference increased to 0.08 MPa, the value of J was much higher for spiral membrane than for flat membrane.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: High operating pressure difference (0.1 MPa) was required for flat membrane to reach the same permeance level as the spiral one, because of the higher linear velocity (high Reynolds number, thin diffusion layer) in flat membrane test. These values are similar to the values reported by Obuskovic [3]. Permeance was relatively stable under the same trans-membrane pressure, as it did not change much when toluene concentrations in the feed gas changed in the test range.
texts.text: High operating pressure difference (0.1 MPa) was required for flat membrane to reach the same permeance level as the spiral one, because of the higher linear velocity (high Reynolds number, thin diffusion layer) in flat membrane test. These values are similar to the values reported by Obuskovic [3]. Permeance was relatively stable under the same trans-membrane pressure, as it did not change much when toluene concentrations in the feed gas changed in the test range.

Multi-step membrane separation may be chosen to enhance recovery for stringent emission control.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 4. Conclusions
texts.text: 4. Conclusions

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The following conclusions were drawn from this study on separating toluene from air with POMS membrane:
texts.text: The following conclusions were drawn from this study on separating toluene from air with POMS membrane:

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Using only vacuum at the permeate side, the membrane separation efficiency of toluene depends very much on the permeate pressure difference: the higher the /Delta1 P applied, the higher the removal rates reached in the spiral wound module. 0.1 MPa /Delta1 P pressure difference is enough for recovering (>80%) toluene. This is true for separating and recovering toluene from air using both flat and spiral mode membrane.
texts.text: Using only vacuum at the permeate side, the membrane separation efficiency of toluene depends very much on the permeate pressure difference: the higher the /Delta1 P applied, the higher the removal rates reached in the spiral wound module. 0.1 MPa /Delta1 P pressure difference is enough for recovering (>80%) toluene. This is true for separating and recovering toluene from air using both flat and spiral mode membrane.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · For recovering 1500-4000mg/m 3 toluene in air, using POMS membrane, a comparatively high removal rate (84%) could be achieved by only vacuuming in the permeate side, and the variation in toluene concentration did not influence the removal rate or the permeance greatly when /Delta1 P was fixed.
texts.text: For recovering 1500-4000mg/m 3 toluene in air, using POMS membrane, a comparatively high removal rate (84%) could be achieved by only vacuuming in the permeate side, and the variation in toluene concentration did not influence the removal rate or the permeance greatly when /Delta1 P was fixed.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The permeance increases in proportional to TMP. Under certain /Delta1 P , permeance J was a relatively steady value. It did not change greatly with the variation of feed flow rate in the test range and condition. The measured permeance for toluene in POMS membrane was 1.30-8.01 × 10 -7 mols -1 m 2 Pa, and this offered basic data for larger scale separation operations where similar condition might be adopted.
texts.text: The permeance increases in proportional to TMP. Under certain /Delta1 P , permeance J was a relatively steady value. It did not change greatly with the variation of feed flow rate in the test range and condition. The measured permeance for toluene in POMS membrane was 1.30-8.01 × 10 -7 mols -1 m 2 Pa, and this offered basic data for larger scale separation operations where similar condition might be adopted.
